_metadata.purpose: Per-entry provenance for all keywords and patterns proposed in s3g_proposed_keywords.json and s3g_proposed_patterns.json
_metadata.note: These providers were identified through a coverage comparison exercise against open-source secret scanners (trufflehog, gitleaks). All content — keyword names and regex patterns — is sourced independently from public provider documentation and common industry knowledge. No code, regex, or test data was copied from any scanner.

keyword_provenance.default_source: provider documentation and public provider name knowledge

replicate_api_key.format_description: r8_ prefix + 40 alphanumeric characters; format confirmed in official auth docs and SDK source

xai_api_key.source: xAI official documentation — docs.x.ai/api; xai- prefix confirmed in xAI API quickstart

langfuse_public_key.source: Langfuse official SDK documentation — langfuse.com/docs/sdk; pk-lf- prefix is the canonical public key prefix
langfuse_public_key.format_description: pk-lf- prefix + 32+ alphanumeric/hyphen characters; public key used as LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY env var

langfuse_secret_key.source: Langfuse official SDK documentation — langfuse.com/docs/sdk; sk-lf- prefix is the canonical secret key prefix
langfuse_secret_key.format_description: sk-lf- prefix + 32+ alphanumeric/hyphen characters; secret key used as LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY env var

posthog_api_key.source: PostHog official developer documentation — posthog.com/docs/api; phc_ prefix for project API keys, phx_ prefix for personal API keys

not_added_rationale.deepseek: Uses sk- prefix identical to OpenAI format. Existing openai_legacy_key pattern (sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{32,}) already covers this shape. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.mongodb_connection_string: mongodb:// connection strings already covered by existing connection_string pattern in default_patterns.json (regex: (?i)(?:mysql|postgres|postgresql|mongodb|redis|amqp|mssql)://[^\s]+). Not duplicated.
not_added_rationale.postgresql_connection_string: postgres:// and postgresql:// already covered by existing connection_string pattern. Not duplicated.
not_added_rationale.snowflake_connection_string: Snowflake uses JDBC/ODBC connection strings with account.snowflakecomputing.com URIs. Not covered by existing connection_string pattern, but the format is not a simple prefixed token — it embeds hostname structure. Keyword entries (snowflake, snowflake_password, snowflake_account) are sufficient for column-name detection. A Snowflake JDBC regex could be a Sprint 13 item if false-negative evidence emerges.
not_added_rationale.wandb_token: W&B API keys (wandb.ai) are 40-character hex strings (historical) or bearer tokens with no distinctive prefix. The local- prefix is for local server installs only. No distinctive prefix for cloud tokens — keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.elevenlabs: ElevenLabs keys use an xi-api-key header but the token value itself (sk_ prefix followed by alphanumeric) is not publicly confirmed as having a fixed-length format in official docs. Keyword entry sufficient pending confirmation.
not_added_rationale.segment: Segment write keys are opaque 40-char strings with no confirmed distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.salesforce: Salesforce OAuth tokens (access_token, refresh_token) are opaque bearer tokens with no distinctive prefix. Connected App credentials are client_id + client_secret opaque strings. Keyword entries (salesforce, sfdc) are sufficient.
not_added_rationale.langsmith: LangSmith API keys use ls__api_ prefix in some SDK versions but this is not consistently documented as stable. Keyword entry sufficient; add prefix pattern if confirmed.
not_added_rationale.mixpanel: Mixpanel project tokens and API secrets are opaque alphanumeric strings with no distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.klaviyo: Klaviyo private keys have pk_ prefix in some versions but format changed with API v2022-10-17. Keyword entry sufficient pending stable format confirmation.
not_added_rationale.nvidia: NVIDIA NGC API keys are opaque strings with no distinctive prefix in public documentation. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.braintree: Braintree merchant ID + public/private key are opaque alphanumeric strings. No distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.razorpay: Razorpay keys use rzp_live_ and rzp_test_ prefixes — these ARE distinctive but Razorpay is primarily India-market and existing secret-dict likely covers this already. Filed as potential Sprint 13 addition after corpus validation.
not_added_rationale.ringcentral: RingCentral uses OAuth 2.0 tokens — opaque bearer tokens, no distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.opsgenie: OpsGenie API keys are UUIDs. UUID pattern already exists in default_patterns.json. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.clickup: ClickUp personal tokens use pk_ prefix but not consistently documented. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.amplitude: Amplitude API key + secret key are opaque hex strings. No distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.bugsnag: Bugsnag API key is a 32-char hex string. No distinctive prefix. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.monday: monday.com API tokens are opaque JWT-format strings. JWT pattern already exists. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.telnyx: Telnyx API keys use KEY prefix in some docs but not consistently. Keyword entry sufficient.
not_added_rationale.vonage_nexmo: Vonage/Nexmo uses API key + API secret pair; both are opaque alphanumeric strings (~8 chars each). No distinctive prefix. Keyword entries sufficient.
not_added_rationale.posthog_project_api_key: Note: phc_ is project API key (embeddable, less sensitive — used in frontend). phx_ is personal API key (more sensitive). Both captured by ph[cx]_ regex. The project API key (phc_) is safe to embed in frontend code by design — consider whether phc_ should have lower confidence than phx_ in a future refinement.
